role: A React presentation component that renders a build-intent review card in the inbox/surface UI, surfacing lifecycle phase, operator-facing outcome, blast-radius reach, context bundle, and the demonstrate-first affordance.
capabilities: Render build-intent card with phase-derived CSS class and data-ui-ref
Display consequence class and declared scope metadata
Show the original intent/spec for awaiting or running builds
Render persisted blast-radius reach with navigation for awaiting builds
Render surfaced context bundle for awaiting builds
Present demonstrate-first outcome and a "show me" button for done builds
Provide a collapsible changed-files record, build summary, and permission mode
substance_why: The file fully implements conditional phase-based rendering, integrates two child components, wires multiple user actions, and includes extensive inline design rationale.
interactions_present: Head click calls onOpen(d.id) to compile/open the decision
BlastRadiusReach receives onNavigate for address navigation
ContextBundle receives onNavigate for context navigation/pinning
Record disclosure toggle switches showRecord state
interactions_missing: FORM = needs-tim stubs for BlastRadiusReach and ContextBundle placement/approval UI
No explicit approve/reject affordance beyond head-click compile
No internal error boundary or loading state
No fallback when onNavigate is undefined
performance_risks: Potentially large changed_files array is mapped on every render without memoization
deriveOutcome, buildPhase, and class-string concatenation run every render
New closures created for every onClick handler on each render
reuse_verdict_why: The card is a well-scoped pure-presentational container whose concepts and phase logic should be preserved, but its layout and child wiring should be rebuilt for the redesign.
decomposition_hints: Split phase-specific bodies into separate subcomponents: AwaitingReachPanel, DoneOutcomePanel, SurfacedBackPanel
Extract the collapsible record section into its own RecordDisclosure component
Keep the card as a thin orchestrator that selects the phase panel and delegates navigation/demonstration callbacks
